items.title: F-001: Open File
items.intent: Users must be able to open any markdown file from any location via the system file picker — no vault, no import, no friction
items.description: Open any markdown file via UIDocumentPickerViewController. Filters to markdown extensions (.md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd, .mkdn, .mdx) per D-FILE-6. UTF-8 only (D-FILE-2). Error on non-UTF-8. Warning on >1MB. Editable immediately. File open to editing ready <500ms for <100KB files. Depends on: FEAT-040 (File Coordination Layer).
acceptanceCriteria: User opens a .md file from iCloud Drive, edits it, and the edit is saved back to the same file in place
Non-UTF-8 file shows error without crashing or corrupting
File >1MB shows dismissable warning
VoiceOver announces file opened with filename
Security-scoped bookmark persists — file reopens on next launch without re-picking
Opening a file already open in another editor window shows that window instead of duplicating
File provider going offline mid-session preserves in-memory content and shows non-modal error

items.title: F-002: Create File
items.intent: Users must be able to create new markdown files anywhere they have write access — no app-specific folders, no restrictions
items.description: Create new .md file via system file picker save dialog. LF line endings. Default filename Untitled.md. File immediately opened for editing after creation.
acceptanceCriteria: User creates a new .md file in any accessible location and begins writing immediately
New file uses LF line endings
Default filename is Untitled.md, user can rename in picker
User cancels save picker and returns to previous state with no side effects
Write failure (disk full, permissions) shows error with no corrupt or empty file left behind
Creating a file with an existing name at that location prompts for overwrite or rename

items.title: F-003a: Rich Text Rendering (Core)
items.intent: Core markdown-to-styled-text rendering: headings, bold, italic, lists, blockquotes, code spans. The foundation that makes writing feel beautiful. Tables, images, interactive elements, and source toggle are separate items.
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-038 (Parser), FEAT-039 (Text Engine). Render AST nodes as attributed text: 6 heading levels, bold/italic/strikethrough inline, ordered/unordered lists with indentation, blockquotes with left border, code spans with monospace+background, horizontal rules, paragraph spacing. Performance: <16ms render, 120fps scroll. Malformed markdown renders best-effort.
acceptanceCriteria: User writes markdown and sees styled output in real time
Toggling to source shows valid markdown, round-trip preserves content perfectly
CJK, RTL text, accented characters, and emoji render and edit correctly
VoiceOver navigates document structure semantically
Dynamic Type scales editor content
All 6 heading levels render at distinct sizes and weights
Bold, italic, strikethrough render inline with syntax chars hidden in rich view
Nested lists render with correct indentation up to 4 levels
Keystroke-to-render under 16ms measured via Instruments on iPhone 15
5000-line doc scrolls at 120fps on ProMotion with zero dropped frames
Malformed markdown renders best-effort without crash or blank screen

items.title: F-004a: Auto-Format Lists
items.intent: List auto-formatting is the first-keystroke wow moment — Enter continues the list, renumbering just works, indentation is always correct.
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine), FEAT-038 (Parser). Enter in list item continues with correct marker and indentation. Enter on empty list item ends the list. Ordered lists auto-renumber on add/remove/reorder. Nested lists auto-correct to 2 or 4 spaces (configurable in settings). Tab/Shift-Tab indent/outdent list items. Haptic feedback on continuation. Each operation is discrete undo step. Does NOT fire inside code blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: User types a list item and presses Enter — list continues with correct marker and indentation
Ordered lists auto-renumber when items added/removed/reordered
Each auto-format action is independently undoable with Cmd+Z
Haptic feedback confirms list auto-continuation
Enter on empty list item ends the list and removes the empty marker
Auto-format does NOT fire inside fenced code blocks
Tab indents a list item one level, Shift-Tab outdents
Performance: auto-format completes within same frame as keystroke on a 500-item list

items.title: F-005: Document Doctor
items.intent: The editor must proactively catch structural issues in markdown files — broken links, heading hierarchy, formatting problems — without interrupting the writing flow
items.description: Background analysis with inline indicators. MVP rules: broken relative links, heading hierarchy skips, duplicate headings, trailing whitespace, missing blank lines between blocks. Subtle underline/margin indicators (shape+color, not color alone). Tap for popover with Fix/Dismiss. Per-file per-session dismissals. Non-blocking. Haptic on fix. Evaluates within 1s of open, re-evaluates within 500ms after edits pause.
acceptanceCriteria: Doctor indicators appear within 1 second of opening a file with structural issues
User taps indicator, sees suggestion, can accept fix or dismiss
Dismissals persist for the session but not across file closes
Doctor never prevents writing or saving
VoiceOver announces each issue when navigating to indicators
Doctor does not flag valid but unusual markdown as an issue (low false positive rate)
Doctor runs in both rich text and source view
Doctor evaluates a 3000-line document within 2 seconds

items.title: F-006: Syntax Highlighting
items.intent: Code blocks must look good — syntax highlighting makes markdown files with code feel professional and readable
items.description: Fenced code blocks with language detection from info string. Min languages: Python, JS/TS, Swift, Go, Rust, HTML/CSS, JSON, YAML, SQL, Bash, Ruby, Java, Kotlin, C/C++, PHP. Monospace font, background differentiation. Editable inline. Colors adapt to dark/light theme.
acceptanceCriteria: User writes a fenced code block with a language tag — code is syntax-highlighted correctly
Highlighting adapts correctly in both light and dark modes
Unknown or unsupported language tag falls back to plain monospace, no broken rendering
Empty code block renders correctly with background but no content
Code block inside a blockquote renders with both blockquote border and code background

items.title: F-007: Dark and Light Mode
items.intent: The app must look intentionally designed in both dark and light modes — not just inverted colors, but a considered palette for each
items.description: Defaults to system preference. User can override to always-light or always-dark. All UI elements, editor chrome, rendered markdown, and syntax highlighting adapt. All themes meet WCAG AA contrast ratios. Theme transitions animated (crossfade 200ms) with Reduced Motion alternative (instant switch).
acceptanceCriteria: Toggling iOS dark mode causes the app to switch immediately
All text, backgrounds, and UI elements are legible and intentionally designed in both modes
Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA in both modes
Theme override preference persists across app updates
Dynamic theme switch during editing causes no text loss, no cursor jump
Theme transition animates with 200ms crossfade between modes
Reduced Motion setting triggers instant switch instead of animated crossfade

items.title: F-008: Auto-Save
items.intent: Users must never lose work — saving is automatic, continuous, and invisible
items.description: Save triggers: 1s after last keystroke (debounced), on app background, on file close. Uses file coordination APIs. No save button/indicator/confirmation. Non-modal error with retry on failure. Subtle haptic pulse on background save.
acceptanceCriteria: User edits a file, switches to another app, switches back — all changes preserved
User force-quits the app — content saved up to last debounce is preserved
Save failure shows non-modal error with retry option
External file change during editing triggers conflict detection (FEAT-045)
Auto-save on a 500KB file completes within 100ms with no perceptible input lag
Disk full during save shows non-modal error and retains in-memory content
Subtle haptic pulse confirms background save when app is foregrounded

items.title: F-009: Keyboard Support
items.intent: iPad with external keyboard must be a first-class writing experience — every action reachable without touching the screen
items.description: Text editing: Cmd+B/I/K, Cmd+Shift+K. Navigation: Cmd+Up/Down, Option+arrows, Cmd+arrows. App: Cmd+O/N/W, Cmd+Shift+P (source toggle). AI: Cmd+J. Cmd-hold shows shortcut overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: User with external keyboard performs all common editing, navigation, and AI tasks without touching the screen
Cmd-hold shows keyboard shortcut overlay
All 15+ documented shortcuts (Cmd+B/I/K, Cmd+O/N/W, Cmd+J, Cmd+Shift+P, etc.) work in both rich and source view
No shortcuts conflict with iOS system shortcuts
Cmd-hold overlay lists ALL registered shortcuts organized by category

items.title: F-010: Typography and Layout
items.intent: Typography is our silent differentiator — the rendered markdown must be so beautiful that users notice it subconsciously and can never go back to other editors
items.description: Custom typefaces for editor and UI chrome. Line height, paragraph spacing, margins optimized per device class. Responsive layout adapts to screen size. Max content width on large screens (~70-80 chars). Respects Dynamic Type.
acceptanceCriteria: Designer reviews rendered markdown on iPhone SE, iPhone 15 Pro Max, iPad Mini, and iPad Pro — all feel intentionally designed
Dynamic Type at default and large sizes renders correctly on all device classes
Content width is 65-80 characters on iPad landscape and external displays
Line height is 1.5-1.7x font size, paragraph spacing is at least 0.5x line height
Minimum margins: 16pt on iPhone, 32pt on iPad
Custom typeface renders correctly for Latin, CJK, Arabic, and Devanagari scripts
Extreme Dynamic Type accessibility sizes render without clipping or text overlap

items.title: F-025a: AI Improve Writing
items.intent: Select text, tap Improve, get a better version. This is the primary AI interaction and the one we demo to sell the product.
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline), FEAT-054 (AI Action Bar). Select text, see Improve in floating action bar, tap it. AI produces improved version with grammar, clarity, and conciseness fixes. Shows inline diff preview: original with strikethrough, suggestion in green. User taps Accept (text replaced, haptic), edits suggestion, or dismisses. Undoable with Cmd+Z. Streams progressively. First token <500ms, full response <3s. Works offline. AI is content-aware: understands whether selection is prose, a diagram block, or a table, and adapts its suggestions accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: User selects a paragraph, taps Improve, sees inline diff within 3 seconds, accepts with one tap
AI works in airplane mode with no network connection
AI works on iPhone 15 and does not appear on iPhone 14
All AI actions are undoable with Cmd+Z
Inline diff preview shows original and suggestion clearly distinguishable
Accept replaces text and triggers haptic confirmation
Dismissing returns to original text with no modifications
AI output streams token-by-token into the diff preview

items.title: F-033: Word Count and Document Stats
items.intent: Writers need to see their progress at a glance — word count and reading time must be always visible without being intrusive
items.description: Compact status bar at bottom: word count, character count (with/without spaces), estimated reading time. Updates in real time. Tap to expand: paragraph count, sentence count, Flesch-Kincaid readability. Selection-aware: shows selection count alongside total.
acceptanceCriteria: User sees word count and reading time in status bar at all times while writing
Selecting text updates stats to show selection count alongside total
VoiceOver can read current word count and reading time via status bar
Word count updates cause no perceptible input lag (under 1ms overhead per keystroke)
CJK text word count uses appropriate segmentation (not space-delimited)
Status bar does not overlap editor content on iPhone SE screen size
Tapping the status bar expands to show paragraph count, sentence count, and Flesch-Kincaid readability score

items.title: F-034: Spell Check
items.intent: Spell check is table stakes for any writing tool — it must work out of the box using the system spell checker
items.description: Uses UITextChecker (iOS) / NSSpellChecker (macOS). Red underline on misspelled words. Tap/right-click for correction suggestions. Respects system language and custom dictionary. Coexists with document doctor indicators without visual conflict. Works in both rich text and source view. On by default, toggleable.
acceptanceCriteria: User types a misspelled word — red underline appears
Tapping the underlined word shows correction suggestions
Spell check works in both rich text and source view
Spell check and doctor indicators do not visually conflict
Spell check does NOT flag words inside code blocks, code spans, or URLs
Spell check works for the users system language, not just English
Custom dictionary additions persist across app launches

items.title: F-035: The Render (Signature Transition)
items.intent: The source-to-rich transition is our brand — a designed animation that makes people say 'whoa' and toggle it just to watch. This is the show-someone moment.
items.description: When opening a file or toggling source to rich: # markers shrink as headings scale up, ** dissolves as text boldfaces, list markers morph into styled bullets, code fences fade as blocks materialize, links compact to styled form, blockquote markers become visual borders. ~400ms spring-animated. Reverse plays on rich-to-source. Reduced Motion: instant crossfade 200ms. Uses Core Animation for guaranteed 120fps on ProMotion. VoiceOver unaffected — announces rendered content immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-technical observer watching over the shoulder finds the transition visually striking
Animation runs at 120fps on ProMotion devices with no dropped frames
Reduced Motion setting triggers instant crossfade instead
User toggles source/rich multiple times because the animation is delightful
Animation duration is 350-450ms using spring curve with damping 0.8 and response 0.4
Every element type has an interpolated transition (no elements pop in or out)
Reverse animation rich-to-source plays at equal quality to forward
Toggle latency (tap to first animation frame) under 50ms
Works correctly on empty document and on 1-line document
Rapid toggle (tap 5 times quickly) does not crash or produce glitched state
The Render animation plays when opening a file (not just on source toggle)

items.title: F-036a: iPad Multi-Window and Stage Manager
items.intent: iPad multi-window is what makes easy-markdown a serious writing tool — two documents side by side, or easy-markdown next to Safari for research
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-037 (App Shell). Full Stage Manager support: resizable windows, multiple instances. UIScene-based lifecycle. Each window is an independent editor with its own file. Memory managed across windows. Minimum iPad: iPad Air 4th gen (M1 for Stage Manager, but basic multi-window on older iPads via Split View).
acceptanceCriteria: iPad Pro user with Magic Keyboard and external display uses easy-markdown as primary writing environment for a full day
Stage Manager multi-window works with two easy-markdown documents side by side
Split View at 1/3, 1/2, and 2/3 widths reflows content gracefully
Trackpad hover states appear on doctor indicators, AI action bar, and toolbar buttons
Two easy-markdown windows open side by side in Stage Manager with independent files
No crashes or memory warnings during 2-hour session with 2 windows open
Closing one window does not affect the other

items.title: F-011: Quick Open
items.intent: Power users need instant file switching — fuzzy find across recent files must be faster than the system file picker
items.description: Cmd+P or swipe gesture to invoke. Searches recent files and bookmarked directories. Fuzzy matching on filename and path. Results ranked by recency and match quality. Results within 200ms of typing 3 characters.
acceptanceCriteria: User presses Cmd+P, types 3 characters, correct file appears in top 3 results within 200ms
Empty state (no recent files) shows a helpful message, not a blank screen
Recents entry for deleted/inaccessible file is excluded from results

items.title: F-012: Find and Replace
items.intent: In-document search and replace is essential for editing longer documents — must support regex for power users
items.description: Cmd+F to invoke find bar. Plain text and regex modes. Match highlighting in document. Replace one / replace all. Case-sensitive toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: User searches for a regex pattern in a 500-line document — all matches highlighted
Replace-all correctly substitutes all occurrences
Replace-all is a single undo step
Search in a 5000-line document returns results within 200ms
Invalid regex shows a clear error message, does not crash

items.title: F-013: Render, Print, and Share
items.intent: Share markdown with the world — as a .md file via share sheet, as a beautiful print, or as a rendered PDF. Markdown is the primary format; rendering is for handoff to non-markdown users.
items.description: Share as markdown: iOS share sheet for .md file (AirDrop, email, Messages, any app). Save to new location: copy .md to different directory/cloud/external storage. Print: rendered rich text matching in-editor appearance. Rendered PDF: polished PDF for sharing with non-markdown users. No Word, Pages, RTF, or proprietary format export (D-NO-12).
acceptanceCriteria: User exports a document with headings, lists, code blocks, and images to PDF — result looks like a polished document
PDF export includes images from relative paths
User can save/copy the .md file to a different directory or cloud drive
User shares .md file via AirDrop to a colleague
User prints document and output matches in-editor rich text appearance
Rendered PDF includes images and code blocks with syntax highlighting
No proprietary format options appear anywhere in the export flow (no Word, Pages, RTF, HTML)

items.title: F-014: Custom Themes and Fonts
items.intent: Personalization deepens attachment to the tool — users should be able to make easy-markdown feel like theirs
items.description: 3-5 built-in color themes with light and dark variants each. Font selection from curated list (we control quality). Font size adjustment. Theme preview before committing. Settings persist via UserDefaults. All themes meet WCAG AA contrast ratios.
acceptanceCriteria: User selects a different theme and font — entire editor updates immediately
Selection persists across app launches
All themes pass WCAG AA contrast check
Theme preview shows the effect on editor content before committing the selection

items.title: F-015: Image Handling
items.intent: Working with images in markdown should be effortless — drag, drop, done
items.description: Drop/paste image into editor prompts for save location, inserts relative path markdown. Images rendered inline in rich text mode. Broken image paths shown with placeholder and doctor warning (F-005).
acceptanceCriteria: User drags image into editor — saved to chosen location, relative markdown link inserted, renders inline
Broken image paths show placeholder with doctor warning
Pasting an image from clipboard prompts for save location and inserts relative path
Very large image (10MB+) shows progress during save, does not freeze the editor

items.title: F-016: macOS App
items.intent: macOS must feel like a native Mac app — not a port. Mac users are discerning and will reject anything that feels like iOS on a bigger screen.
items.description: Native macOS window management (tabs, split view, full screen). Menu bar with complete keyboard shortcut coverage. Drag-and-drop file opening. Services menu integration. Finder Quick Look preview for .md files. Touch Bar support if applicable.
acceptanceCriteria: macOS app passes Apple HIG review
A Mac-native user would not guess it was ported from iOS
Quick Look preview works for .md files in Finder
Uses NSWindow, not UIWindow via Catalyst
Menu bar has standard File/Edit/View/Format/Window/Help menus
Cmd+comma opens preferences, Cmd+Q quits
Right-click context menu matches system style and includes AI actions on selected text

items.title: F-017: Extended Document Doctor
items.intent: Deepen writing intelligence beyond structural checks — help users write better prose, not just better markdown
items.description: Readability scoring (Flesch-Kincaid or similar) in status bar. Writing goals: user sets target word count, progress shown visually. Prose suggestions (opt-in): flag very long sentences, passive voice, repeated words.
acceptanceCriteria: User enables prose suggestions — doctor flags a 60-word sentence with suggestion to break it up
User can accept suggestion (cursor positioned for edit) or dismiss
Writing goal progress is shown visually as user approaches target word count

items.title: F-026: AI — Tone and Style Adjustment (Pro AI)
items.intent: Users need to adapt their writing tone for different audiences — Pro AI delivers quality tone shifts that exceed local model capability
items.description: Select text, adjust tone: more formal, more casual, more technical, simpler. 4 preset tone adjustments + custom instruction option. Same inline diff preview as F-025. Appears in floating action bar with Pro badge. Non-subscribers see tasteful upgrade prompt. Only selected text sent to cloud per D-AI-8.
acceptanceCriteria: Pro subscriber selects casual paragraph, taps More Formal, sees rewritten version within 3 seconds, accepts with one tap
Non-subscriber sees a non-intrusive explanation of Pro AI with subscribe option
Only selected text is sent to cloud API
Cloud API timeout after 10 seconds shows error and suggests using local AI Improve instead
Malformed API response does not crash — shows generic error and preserves original text

items.title: F-027: AI — Translation (Pro AI)
items.intent: Translation is a killer Pro AI feature — select text, pick a language, get an inline preview. Must work across 20+ languages.
items.description: Select text, translate to another language. Cloud-powered for quality across 20+ languages: EN, ES, FR, DE, ZH, JA, KO, PT, IT, RU, AR, HI, NL, SV, PL, DA, NO, FI, TR, TH. Translation appears as inline replacement preview. Original recoverable via undo. Only selected text sent per D-AI-8.
acceptanceCriteria: Pro subscriber selects English paragraph, taps Translate to Spanish, translated text appears as preview within 3 seconds
Undo restores original English text
Only selected text is sent to cloud API
Cloud API failure mid-translation shows partial result with option to retry or cancel
Very long selection (5000+ words) shows warning about processing time

items.title: F-028: AI — Smart Completions (Local AI)
items.intent: Context-aware autocomplete should understand markdown structure — suggest table layouts, list continuations, and patterns, not just words
items.description: Completes markdown structures: table header suggests column layout, list suggests next items based on pattern. Completes front matter patterns if present (read-only per DP-6). Ghost text, Tab to accept. On-device, works offline. Same device floor as F-025: iPhone 15 / M1 Mac minimum.
acceptanceCriteria: User types table header row and presses Enter — AI suggests separator row and first data row
User Tabs to accept ghost text suggestion
Works offline on supported devices, not available on older devices

items.title: F-018: Android App
items.intent: Expand to Android to serve the broader market — must be native Kotlin, not a lowest-common-denominator port
items.description: Native Android app using Kotlin with native UI. Shared logic via KMP or Rust. Must feel native to Android — Material Design, Android navigation patterns, system file picker integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Android app feels native — an Android user would not guess it was ported from iOS
Opens any .md file via Android file picker
Local AI works on supported Android devices

items.title: F-019: Linux Desktop App
items.intent: Linux developers are our people — they write markdown daily, they value open formats, and they are completely underserved by native markdown editors
items.description: Native Linux desktop app. Tech approach: evaluate GTK4 with Rust or Kotlin Multiplatform. Shared logic (parser, formatter, AI) from Swift codebase via Rust bridge or rewrite. Must feel native to Linux — follows GNOME HIG or provides distro-appropriate theming. Full file system access. Local AI via llama.cpp or ONNX Runtime (no Core ML on Linux).
acceptanceCriteria: App feels native on Linux — follows GNOME HIG or provides distro-appropriate theming
Opens any markdown file (.md, .markdown, .mdown, .mkd, .mkdn, .mdx) via native file dialog
App launches and runs natively on Ubuntu 24.04 and Fedora 40
Local AI runs on Linux with NVIDIA or AMD GPU

items.title: F-020: Folder Browsing Sidebar
items.intent: Opt-in convenience for users who work within a project folder — never required, never a vault
items.description: Optional sidebar showing contents of a bookmarked directory. Not a vault — just a file browser convenience. Opt-in, not shown by default. User can bookmark multiple directories. Never required for any workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: User bookmarks a folder and sees its .md files in a sidebar
Sidebar is opt-in, not shown by default
All workflows work without the sidebar

items.title: F-021: Snippet/Template System
items.intent: Let users insert predefined markdown structures quickly — meeting notes, blog post scaffolds, table templates
items.description: Insert predefined markdown structures from a template library. Ship with a few built-in templates. Users can create custom snippets. Invoked via command palette or keyboard shortcut.
acceptanceCriteria: User invokes snippet picker and inserts a predefined markdown structure
User can create and save custom snippets

items.title: F-022a: Mermaid Diagram Rendering
items.intent: AI agents and builders produce documents with diagrams constantly. If a Mermaid block renders as raw text, the document experience is broken. Diagrams must render beautifully inline.
items.description: Render Mermaid diagram blocks as visual diagrams inline in the editor. Fenced code blocks with mermaid language tag render as visual diagrams in rich view. Supported types: flowcharts, sequence, ER, class, Gantt, pie, state, mind maps. Diagrams adapt to light/dark theme. Included in PDF/print export as vector graphics. The Render animation applies. Malformed Mermaid shows raw code with doctor warning. Depends on: FEAT-038 (Parser).
acceptanceCriteria: User opens a file with a Mermaid flowchart — it renders as a visual diagram inline
All 8 supported Mermaid diagram types render correctly
Diagram adapts to light/dark theme
Malformed Mermaid shows raw code with doctor warning
Toggle to source shows Mermaid code, toggle back re-renders diagram
PDF export includes rendered diagrams as vector graphics
Diagram renders within 500ms of file open
VoiceOver reads diagram type and text labels

items.title: F-023: Shortcuts/Siri Integration
items.intent: Deep Apple platform integration — let users create new notes, open recent files, and trigger actions via Shortcuts and Siri
items.description: iOS Shortcuts integration: create new file, open recent file, open specific file. Siri: voice commands for common actions. Requires iOS/macOS apps to be shipped and stable first.
acceptanceCriteria: User creates a Shortcut that opens a specific .md file in easy-markdown
Siri can create a new markdown file via voice command

items.title: F-024: Quick Capture Widget
items.intent: Capture a thought instantly from the home screen without opening the app — lowest friction path to a new note
items.description: iOS home screen widget for quick capture. Tap to create a new markdown file and start typing immediately. Requires iOS app to be shipped and stable.
acceptanceCriteria: User taps home screen widget and is editing a new markdown file within 2 seconds
Widget works without the app being open in the foreground

items.title: F-029: AI — Generate from Prompt (Pro AI)
items.intent: Let users describe what they want and have AI generate the markdown — README scaffolds, comparison tables, outlines
items.description: Cloud-powered Pro AI feature. User types a prompt like 'write a README for a React component library' or 'create a table comparing AWS vs GCP storage'. AI generates markdown inserted at cursor. Inline preview before accepting.
acceptanceCriteria: User types a generation prompt and receives structured markdown output within 5 seconds
Generated content appears as preview before being inserted

items.title: F-030: AI — Document-Level Analysis (Pro AI)
items.intent: Let users ask the AI about their entire document — what is missing, suggest an outline, identify gaps in coverage
items.description: Cloud-powered Pro AI feature. User can ask document-level questions: 'what is missing from this doc?', 'suggest an outline', 'identify gaps'. Requires sending document content to cloud with explicit user consent per D-AI-8.
acceptanceCriteria: User asks what is missing from their document and receives actionable suggestions
User explicitly consents before document content is sent to cloud

items.title: F-031: AI — OCR + Image-to-Markdown
items.intent: Paste a screenshot and get markdown — tables extracted as markdown tables, text extracted as paragraphs
items.description: Paste screenshot or image, AI extracts text and tables as markdown. Could run locally (vision model) or via Pro AI cloud depending on hardware/model capability. Tables extracted as markdown tables, text as paragraphs.
acceptanceCriteria: User pastes a screenshot of a table — AI extracts it as a formatted markdown table
User pastes a screenshot of text — AI extracts it as markdown paragraphs

items.title: F-032: UI Localization
items.intent: The defacto global markdown editor needs to speak the user's language — localize the app interface to top 10 languages
items.description: Translate app interface (menus, buttons, settings, error messages, doctor suggestions) to top 10 languages. Content editing already supports all languages per D-USER-4. This is UI chrome localization only.
acceptanceCriteria: App interface displays correctly in all 10 supported languages
All strings including doctor suggestions and AI action labels are localized

items.title: INFRA: App Shell and Navigation
items.intent: The container for the entire app — launch screen, home screen, toolbar, status bar, settings gear. Nothing can be built without the shell.
items.description: Basic app structure: LaunchScreen, home screen with Open File / New File buttons (per D-UX-1 — no onboarding). Top toolbar (format actions, source toggle, settings gear). Bottom status bar (word count slot, doctor indicator count). Navigation between home, editor, and settings. SwiftUI-based per D-PLAT-2. iOS 17+ per D-PLAT-3.
acceptanceCriteria: App launches to home screen with Open File and New File buttons within 1 second (D-PERF-1)
No onboarding, no tutorial, no account creation screens appear on first launch
Toolbar, status bar, and settings gear are visible and tappable in the editor view
VoiceOver can navigate all shell elements
App runs on iOS 17+ and crashes gracefully with a message on iOS 16

items.title: INFRA: Markdown Parser (CommonMark + GFM)
items.intent: The parser is the foundation — every rendering, formatting, doctor, and export feature depends on a correct AST. Get this wrong and everything downstream is broken.
items.description: Integrate or build a CommonMark + GFM compliant parser that produces an AST (per D-MD-1). Must handle: headings, paragraphs, bold/italic/strikethrough, links, images, code spans, fenced code blocks with language info, blockquotes, ordered/unordered lists, task lists, tables, thematic breaks, autolinks. Must handle malformed/incomplete markdown gracefully (partial parse, not crash). Must be fast: parse a 10,000-line document in <100ms. Evaluate: swift-markdown, cmark-gfm Swift wrapper, or custom. AST must support round-trip (AST → markdown preserves original formatting). AST must include source positions (line:column ranges) for doctor indicators and cursor mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser produces correct AST for all CommonMark + GFM spec examples
Malformed markdown produces a partial AST without crashing
10,000-line document parses in <100ms on iPhone 15
AST round-trips to identical markdown (AST → markdown → AST produces same tree)
AST nodes include source position ranges (line:column start/end)

items.title: INFRA: Text Editing Engine
items.intent: The core text system that handles every keystroke — must be fast enough for <16ms render and flexible enough for rich text, source view, and auto-formatting hooks.
items.description: Core text input and rendering engine. Decision needed: TextKit 2 (modern, SwiftUI-friendly, but less mature) vs UITextView with custom NSAttributedString (proven but harder to customize). Must support: text input and editing, attributed string rendering, cursor/selection management, custom text attributes for markdown styling, hooks for auto-formatting (intercept keystrokes before commit), undo manager integration, IME support for CJK input, RTL text layout, Dynamic Type font scaling. Performance: <16ms keystroke-to-render (D-PERF-2), 120fps scroll (D-PERF-3).
acceptanceCriteria: User types and sees characters appear with <16ms latency (measured via instruments)
Scrolling a 5,000-line document maintains 60fps on all devices, 120fps on ProMotion
CJK IME composition works correctly (partial input displayed, committed on confirm)
RTL text (Arabic, Hebrew) displays and edits with correct cursor behavior
Dynamic Type at all sizes (including accessibility sizes) renders without clipping or overlap
Undo manager tracks all text changes with unlimited depth

items.title: INFRA: File Coordination Layer
items.intent: iOS file access is sandboxed and complex — security-scoped URLs, file coordination, and bookmarking must be rock-solid or users lose access to their files.
items.description: Foundation for all file operations. Security-scoped URL handling: obtain, persist, and resolve bookmarks so files remain accessible across app launches (required for recents). NSFileCoordinator integration for safe reads/writes that respect other processes (iCloud, Dropbox, Git). NSFilePresenter for detecting external file changes (D-FILE-5). UTF-8 encoding validation (D-FILE-2). Line ending detection and preservation (D-FILE-3). File size check and warning (D-FILE-4).
acceptanceCriteria: Security-scoped URL bookmark persists across app launches — file reopens without re-picking
File coordination prevents data corruption when iCloud syncs during a write
External file change triggers a notification to the editor (reload/keep choice)
Non-UTF-8 file detected and error shown without crash or corruption
File with CRLF line endings preserved on save (not converted to LF)
File >1MB triggers dismissable warning before opening

items.title: INFRA: AI Model Pipeline
items.intent: The local AI model must download, load, and run inference reliably — a failed model download or slow inference kills the entire AI value proposition.
items.description: Per D-AI-5 and D-AI-9. Model download: separate from app (<50MB app, ~2-4GB model). Download via ODR or background asset download. Wi-Fi only by default, cellular opt-in. Non-blocking — editor usable during download. Progress indicator. Resume on interruption. Model loading: load into Core ML / MLX on app launch (for capable devices). Memory-mapped to minimize RAM impact. Model inference: token streaming pipeline. First token <500ms (D-PERF-4). Full response <3s. Model update: independent of app updates. Capability detection: check device (A16+/M1+), show/hide AI features accordingly (no broken affordances on unsupported devices).
acceptanceCriteria: Model downloads in background while user edits — no UI blocking
Download interrupted at 50% resumes from where it left off
Cellular download requires explicit user opt-in
Model loads and first inference completes with first token in <500ms on iPhone 15
Memory usage with model loaded stays under 100MB for editing session (D-PERF-5, model may be memory-mapped separately)
iPhone 14 shows no AI-related UI elements (buttons, ghost text, action bar items)
Device running low on storage shows clear message and option to skip/delete model

items.title: INFRA: Settings Screen
items.intent: Minimal but essential — the single place users configure auto-format rules, spell check, AI behavior, theme, and font.
items.description: SwiftUI settings view accessible via gear icon in toolbar. Sections: Appearance (theme, font, font size), Editor (auto-format toggles per rule, spell check toggle, trailing whitespace behavior), AI (ghost text on/off, model management — download/delete), About (version, licenses, support link). Persists via UserDefaults (no sidecar files per DP-1). Opinionated defaults — settings exist to turn things OFF, not to configure complexity.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings screen opens from gear icon in toolbar
Toggling an auto-format rule takes effect immediately in the editor
All settings persist across app launches
VoiceOver can navigate and toggle every setting
Settings screen renders correctly at all Dynamic Type sizes

items.title: INFRA: Recents and State Restoration
items.intent: The app must always return users to where they left off — last file, last cursor position, no friction on relaunch.
items.description: Per D-UX-2: if user had a file open when app closed, reopen it on next launch with cursor at last position. If no last file, show recents list. Recents stored via security-scoped URL bookmarks (depends on File Coordination Layer). Recents list shows: filename, path, last opened date, file size. Max 20 recent entries. Entries removed if file no longer accessible. Recents list is the fallback home screen — not a separate mode.
acceptanceCriteria: User opens a file, backgrounds the app, force-quits, relaunches — same file opens at same cursor position
User with no last file sees recents list with previously opened files
Recents entry for a deleted/moved file is removed gracefully (no crash, no stale entry)
Recents list shows filename, parent folder, and last opened date

items.title: INFRA: First-Run Experience
items.intent: First launch must be instant and self-evident — open the app and you're writing within 30 seconds, with AI model downloading silently in the background.
items.description: Per D-UX-1 (no onboarding) and D-AI-9 (model download). First launch flow: app opens to home screen (Open/New buttons). No splash screen beyond system launch image. On capable devices (A16+/M1+): after 2 seconds, a non-modal banner offers AI model download ('Download AI assistant? ~3GB, Wi-Fi recommended'). User can accept (download starts in background), dismiss (asked again next launch), or go to settings. User can open/create a file immediately regardless of model download state. AI features hidden until model ready.
acceptanceCriteria: First launch to editor-ready state (file open and cursor active) in <30 seconds including user interaction
No onboarding screens, tutorials, or account prompts appear
AI model download prompt appears non-modally and is dismissable
User can edit a file while model downloads in background
On unsupported devices (pre-A16), no AI download prompt appears

items.title: INFRA: File Conflict Detection
items.intent: When a file changes on disk while open in the editor, the user must know — silent data loss from sync conflicts is unacceptable.
items.description: Per D-FILE-5. Uses NSFilePresenter to monitor the open file for external changes. When detected: pause auto-save, show non-modal notification with file diff summary ('This file was modified by another app. Reload external changes or keep your version?'). Options: Reload (discard editor state, load new file), Keep Mine (continue with editor version, next save overwrites), View Diff (show what changed — stretch goal). If file is deleted externally: notify user, offer to save to a new location.
acceptanceCriteria: File modified externally while open triggers a visible notification within 5 seconds
User can choose to reload external changes (editor updates to new content)
User can choose to keep their version (next save overwrites external changes)
File deleted externally triggers notification with option to save elsewhere
No data is silently lost in any conflict scenario

items.title: INFRA: Pro AI Cloud Infrastructure
items.intent: The cloud relay for Pro AI must be minimal, private, and reliable — it routes requests to the AI provider and validates subscriptions, nothing more.
items.description: Lightweight API relay (serverless function or minimal server) that: validates App Store subscription receipt via StoreKit 2 server-side verification, forwards selected text to AI provider (initially Anthropic Claude per D-AI-7), streams response back to client, logs nothing (no prompts, no responses, no user data per D-AI-8). Client-side: StoreKit 2 subscription management, subscription status caching, graceful degradation on network failure (suggest local AI alternative). Privacy pipeline: only user-selected text sent (never full document silently), provider contractual no-retention.
acceptanceCriteria: Pro AI request completes end-to-end (client → relay → provider → client) in <5 seconds
Expired/invalid subscription receipt is rejected with clear user-facing message
Network failure during Pro AI request shows error and suggests using local AI instead
Server logs contain zero user content (prompts, responses, or file data)
Subscription status is cached client-side so the app doesn't block on network for every AI action

items.title: F-003b: Table Rendering
items.intent: Tables must render as formatted, aligned tables in rich view — not as pipe-delimited text
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-038 (Parser), FEAT-003 (Core Rendering). GFM tables render as visually formatted tables with column alignment, cell padding, and header row styling. Tables are editable inline in rich view. Works with auto-format table features (FEAT-004 decomposed).
acceptanceCriteria: GFM table renders with visible column alignment and cell padding
Header row is visually distinct from data rows
Table with 10+ columns renders without horizontal overflow on iPad
Table with 100+ rows scrolls smoothly at 60fps minimum
Empty cells render as empty space, not collapsed

items.title: F-003c: Image Rendering
items.intent: Images referenced in markdown must render inline in rich view — broken paths must show a clear placeholder, not a blank space
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-038 (Parser), FEAT-003 (Core Rendering). Render images from relative and absolute paths inline in rich view. Images scale to fit content width with aspect ratio preserved. Broken/missing image paths show a placeholder icon with the alt text. Supports common formats: PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP, SVG (render as bitmap).
acceptanceCriteria: Image with valid relative path renders inline at appropriate size
Image scales to content width and preserves aspect ratio
Broken image path shows placeholder icon with alt text displayed
Very large image (5000x5000px) does not cause memory spike or lag
GIF renders as static first frame (animated GIF support is P2)

items.title: F-003d: Interactive Elements (Checkboxes and Links)
items.intent: Task list checkboxes must be tappable and links must be clickable — these are the elements that make the rich view feel alive, not static
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-003 (Core Rendering). Task list checkboxes: rendered as interactive checkboxes. Tapping toggles the checkbox AND updates the underlying markdown (- [ ] to - [x]). Links: rendered as styled tappable text. Tap opens URL in system browser. Long-press shows URL preview and copy option. Link text styled with color and subtle underline.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a task list checkbox toggles it and updates the markdown source
Toggling a checkbox is a single undo step
Tapping a link opens the URL in Safari/system browser
Long-pressing a link shows the URL and a copy option
Links to relative files open the file in easy-markdown (if .md) or system handler

items.title: F-003e: Source Toggle with Cursor Mapping
items.intent: Switching between rich and source view must preserve context — the user should land on the same paragraph, not the top of the document
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-003 (Core Rendering), FEAT-038 (Parser). Toggle button in toolbar switches between rich text view and raw markdown source view. Cursor position maps between views using AST source positions — same paragraph, best-effort same line. Changes made in source view are reflected when returning to rich view (re-parse and re-render). Toggle latency (tap to first frame of new view) under 50ms excluding The Render animation. The Render animation (FEAT-014) triggers on this toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggling from rich to source shows raw markdown with cursor at the corresponding line
Toggling from source to rich shows styled view with cursor at the corresponding paragraph
Edits in source view are correctly reflected when toggling back to rich view
Toggle latency under 50ms (excluding Render animation)
Toggle works correctly on an empty document

items.title: F-003f: i18n Text Support (CJK, RTL, Emoji)
items.intent: The editor must handle every writing system correctly — CJK, Arabic, Hebrew, Devanagari, emoji. A global editor that breaks on non-Latin text is not the best in the world.
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine). CJK text: correct rendering, line breaking at character boundaries (not mid-character), IME composition display. RTL text: correct bidirectional layout, cursor moves right-to-left, mixed LTR/RTL in same line. Emoji: correct grapheme cluster handling (cursor treats multi-codepoint emoji as single unit). Accented characters: correct rendering and cursor behavior. All writing systems work in both rich and source view.
acceptanceCriteria: Chinese/Japanese/Korean text renders correctly and line-breaks at character boundaries
Arabic and Hebrew text displays right-to-left with correct cursor direction
Mixed LTR and RTL text in the same line renders with correct bidirectional ordering
Multi-codepoint emoji (flags, skin tones, ZWJ sequences) treated as single cursor unit
IME composition for CJK shows partial input correctly before commit

items.title: F-004b: Auto-Format Tables
items.intent: Tables should format themselves — columns align as you type, Tab moves between cells, new rows appear automatically
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine), FEAT-047 (Table Rendering). Auto-align table columns with space padding as user types. Tab key moves to next cell (or creates new column). Enter in last cell of last row adds a new row. Auto-add missing header separator row when user types a second row. Each operation is discrete undo step. Does NOT fire inside code blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Table columns auto-align with space padding as user types in any cell
Tab moves cursor to next cell, Shift-Tab to previous cell
Enter in last cell of last row creates a new row with correct column count
Missing header separator row is auto-inserted when second row is typed
Each auto-format is independently undoable
Table with 20 columns auto-aligns without perceptible lag

items.title: F-004c: Auto-Format Headings and Whitespace
items.intent: Heading and whitespace formatting keeps documents structurally clean without the user thinking about it
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine), FEAT-038 (Parser). Headings: auto-add space after # markers if missing, normalize ATX style (remove trailing #). Whitespace: trim trailing whitespace when user presses Enter, ensure single blank line between block elements, ensure file ends with newline. Each operation is discrete undo step. Configurable: individual rules toggleable in settings. Does NOT fire inside code blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing ##Heading auto-corrects to ## Heading
Trailing # markers on headings are auto-removed
Pressing Enter trims trailing whitespace from the completed line
Block elements without blank lines between them get a blank line auto-inserted
File always ends with exactly one newline on save
All whitespace rules can be individually toggled off in settings

items.title: F-025b: AI Floating Action Bar
items.intent: The AI action bar is the interaction hub — it must feel like a native extension of text selection, not a separate AI mode
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-039 (Text Engine). When user selects text, a floating action bar appears above the selection. Contains: standard format actions (Bold, Italic, Link) AND AI actions (Improve, Summarize). Pro AI actions (Translate, Tone) appear with Pro badge for subscribers or as discoverable options for non-subscribers. Bar follows selection, dismisses on deselect. Keyboard shortcut Cmd+J focuses the AI section of the bar. Bar adapts to available width. VoiceOver announces all actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Floating bar appears within 200ms of text selection
Bar contains both formatting (Bold, Italic) and AI (Improve, Summarize) actions
Pro AI actions show subtle Pro badge for subscribers
Non-subscriber tapping a Pro action sees a non-intrusive upgrade explanation
Bar dismisses cleanly on deselect with no ghost UI
Cmd+J invokes AI assist when text is selected
VoiceOver can navigate and activate all bar actions

items.title: F-025c: AI Summarize
items.intent: One-tap summarization — select a section or full document and get a concise summary you can insert or copy
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline), FEAT-054 (AI Action Bar). Select text or full document, tap Summarize. AI generates 1-3 sentence summary (for section) or 1 paragraph (for full doc). Summary appears in a popover. User can: Insert at cursor, Copy to clipboard, or Dismiss. Works offline on supported devices. First token <500ms, full response <3s.
acceptanceCriteria: Selected section produces 1-3 sentence summary in a popover within 3 seconds
User can insert summary at cursor position
User can copy summary to clipboard
Works offline on iPhone 15 and newer

items.title: F-025d: AI Continue Writing (Ghost Text)
items.intent: The editor should anticipate what you want to write next — ghost text that appears naturally after a pause and accepts with Tab
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline), FEAT-039 (Text Engine). After a 3-second typing pause at end of text, AI generates 1-3 sentences continuing the thought. Appears as dimmed ghost text inline at cursor. Tab accepts (text inserted, ghost text becomes real). Typing any character dismisses ghost text. Can be toggled off in settings (FEAT-042). Must not conflict with auto-formatting. First token <500ms.
acceptanceCriteria: Ghost text appears after 3-second pause at end of content
Tab key accepts ghost text and it becomes real document text
Typing any character dismisses ghost text immediately
Ghost text does not appear inside code blocks
Ghost text can be toggled off in settings
Ghost text does not trigger auto-formatting rules
VoiceOver announces AI suggestion available when ghost text appears

items.title: F-036b: iPad Split View, Slide Over, and External Display
items.intent: Content must reflow gracefully at every width — from narrow Slide Over to full external display
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-037 (App Shell), FEAT-010 (Typography). Split View at 1/3, 1/2, 2/3 widths with graceful content reflow. Slide Over for quick edits. External display: editor spans full display with content width constraints (70-80 char max per D-PERF typography). No letterboxing. Resolution independent (sharp on non-Retina external displays). Content width adapts in real time during Split View resize.
acceptanceCriteria: Content reflows without jumpiness during Split View resize drag
Slide Over at narrowest width shows content without horizontal scrolling
External display shows sharp text with appropriate content width constraints
No letterboxing or awkward black bars on any display size

items.title: F-036c: iPad Pointer and Trackpad Support
items.intent: iPad with trackpad should feel like a desktop app — hover states, right-click menus, precise cursor placement
items.description: Depends on: FEAT-003 (Core Rendering), FEAT-005 (Doctor). Full trackpad and mouse support via UIPointerInteraction. Hover states on: toolbar buttons, doctor indicators, AI action bar items, links, settings controls. Right-click context menu with: Cut, Copy, Paste, AI actions (if text selected), Look Up. Pointer automatically switches between arrow (UI) and I-beam (text) based on target. Smooth pointer acceleration.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering over a toolbar button shows a highlight/tooltip
Hovering over a doctor indicator shows the issue preview
Right-clicking selected text shows context menu with AI actions
Pointer changes from arrow to I-beam when entering the text editing area

items.title: App Store Review Prompt
items.intent: The review prompt must appear at the right moment — when the user is happy and engaged, never before. One shot, never again.
items.description: Per PRODUCT.md §12. Prompt for App Store review only after: 7 days of active use AND 10+ AI feature uses. Uses SKStoreReviewController. Prompt appears at most once, ever. Timing tracked via on-device aggregate counters (FEAT-060). Never interrupts active editing — shown on return to home screen or after completing an AI action.
acceptanceCriteria: Review prompt does not appear before 7 days of active use AND 10+ AI uses
Review prompt appears at most once per user, ever
Prompt never interrupts active editing
If user has not used AI features, prompt never appears regardless of time

items.title: INFRA: On-Device Aggregate Counters
items.intent: We must know if our AI features are landing without violating privacy — simple local counters tell us enough to make product decisions.
items.description: Per D-BIZ-6. Track simple aggregate counts in UserDefaults: AI improve uses, AI summarize uses, AI continue accepts, doctor fixes accepted, documents opened, days active. Never transmitted off device. Powers App Store review prompt timing (FEAT-069) and status bar stats. No PII, no content, no telemetry — just counts.
acceptanceCriteria: AI feature usage counts increment correctly and persist across launches
Counter data is stored in UserDefaults only — never transmitted
Counters are accessible for review prompt eligibility check (FEAT-069)
Deleting and reinstalling the app resets counters (no external storage)

items.title: Export Watermark (Made with easy-markdown)
items.intent: Every exported PDF is a billboard — a subtle watermark drives organic discovery without being obnoxious.
items.description: Per PRODUCT.md §12. When exporting rendered PDF, include a subtle "Made with easy-markdown" watermark in the footer. User can disable in settings. Tasteful, not tacky — small, light gray, bottom margin. Never appears on printed output unless user explicitly enables it.
acceptanceCriteria: Exported PDF includes subtle footer watermark by default
Watermark can be disabled in settings
Watermark is unobtrusive — small, light gray, does not interfere with content
Print output does not include watermark by default

items.title: Pro AI Subscription Screen and Purchase Flow
items.intent: The subscription screen must be honest, clear, and never pushy — it sells Pro AI on its merits, not through dark patterns.
items.description: Per D-STORE-1, D-BIZ-5, D-BIZ-7, and Journey 6. Subscription screen shown when non-subscriber taps a Pro AI action. Shows: price ($3.99/mo or $29.99/yr), what is included, what happens on cancellation, that the app and local AI keep working without it. Annual plan prominently presented (saves ~37%). Uses StoreKit 2 for purchase flow. No separate account creation. Links to terms of service. No persistent upgrade banners, no nag screens, no feature-shaped holes.
acceptanceCriteria: Subscription screen clearly shows monthly and annual pricing with annual savings highlighted
Screen states what Pro AI includes and what happens on cancellation
Purchase completes via standard StoreKit 2 flow — no separate account creation
After subscribing, Pro AI actions work immediately without app restart
Non-subscriber never sees persistent upgrade banners or nag screens anywhere in the app
Subscription terms satisfy Apple App Store Review Guideline 3.1.2

items.title: INFRA: App Store Purchase and Receipt Validation
items.intent: The $9.99 one-time purchase must just work — StoreKit handles it, we validate the receipt, done.
items.description: Per D-BIZ-1 and D-BIZ-2. Implement StoreKit 2 for the one-time $9.99 app purchase. Receipt validation for purchase verification. Handles: purchase flow, restore purchases, family sharing eligibility. The app must be fully functional after purchase with no additional gates.
acceptanceCriteria: User can purchase the app for $9.99 via standard App Store flow
Purchase receipt validates correctly
Restore purchases works for users who reinstall or switch devices
No additional purchase gates after the initial $9.99 for non-Pro features

items.title: INFRA: Performance Regression Test Suite
items.intent: Performance degrades silently unless measured on every build — automated benchmarks are the only way to keep our performance promises.
items.description: Per D-QA-2. Automated tests that run on every build measuring: cold launch time (D-PERF-1: <1s), keystroke-to-render latency (D-PERF-2: <16ms), scroll FPS (D-PERF-3: 120fps ProMotion), AI first token latency (D-PERF-4: <500ms), memory usage (D-PERF-5: <100MB). A regression that crosses any threshold blocks the build. Uses XCTest performance metrics and Instruments automation.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated benchmarks measure all 5 performance targets on every CI build
Build fails if any performance target is exceeded
Results are logged with historical comparison to detect gradual drift
Tests run on representative device targets (iPhone 15, iPad Pro)

items.title: INFRA: Error Presentation System
items.intent: Every error the user sees must be designed — human-readable, non-modal, recoverable. No raw error codes, no unhandled states.
items.description: Per D-ERR-1 and DP-8. Centralized error presentation layer: non-modal banners for recoverable errors (save failure, network timeout), modal alerts only for data-loss-risk scenarios (file deleted while editing). All error messages are human-written, not technical. Every error includes a recovery action (retry, save elsewhere, dismiss). Error states designed per feature: file I/O errors, AI model errors, network errors, permission errors.
acceptanceCriteria: All error messages are human-readable with no raw error codes or technical jargon
Recoverable errors appear as non-modal banners with retry action
Data-loss-risk errors appear as modal alerts with save/recovery options
No unhandled error state produces a blank screen or crash

items.title: F-022b: AI-Assisted Diagram Editing
items.intent: AI is the interface for editing diagrams — select a diagram, describe a change in natural language, AI rewrites the code and the diagram re-renders. No visual editor needed.
items.description: When user selects or taps a rendered Mermaid diagram, a contextual action bar appears with Edit with AI. User describes change in natural language. AI rewrites the Mermaid code, diagram re-renders. Shows inline diff with rendered preview. Uses same AI infrastructure as F-025. Depends on: FEAT-030 (Mermaid Rendering), FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline).
acceptanceCriteria: User taps a rendered flowchart, describes a change, AI updates the Mermaid code and diagram re-renders
Inline diff shows before/after Mermaid code alongside rendered preview
User can accept, edit manually, or dismiss the AI suggestion
Works with local AI for simple changes, Pro AI for complex modifications
Undo reverts the diagram to its pre-AI state

items.title: F-022c: Extended Syntax Rendering (KaTeX, Footnotes)
items.intent: Users encounter math blocks and footnotes in existing files — we should render them even if we do not generate them
items.description: Render-only support for: KaTeX/LaTeX math blocks (inline and display), footnotes. We render these when present but never generate or auto-format them. No proprietary syntax. Per D-MD-2 Phase 3.
acceptanceCriteria: KaTeX math blocks render as formatted equations inline and in display mode
Footnotes render with superscript references and a footnotes section at bottom
Editor never generates these syntaxes, only renders them

items.title: F-037: Voice Control — Intent-Based Editing
items.intent: Users should be able to speak what they want their document to become — not dictate content, but describe changes. AI interprets the intent and executes. This is hands-free, AI-native document editing.
items.description: Hold mic button (or Cmd+Shift+J) to activate. User speaks an editing intent in natural language. AI interprets and maps to an operation: rewrite for conciseness, add a section, change tone, restructure, generate content at cursor, analyze the document. Shows same inline diff preview as touch/keyboard AI — user accepts, edits, or dismisses. Uses iOS Speech framework (on-device recognition, no network required on iOS 17+). Context-aware: operates on current selection or cursor position. Voice never modifies without confirmation. Depends on: FEAT-041 (AI Model Pipeline), FEAT-054 (AI Action Bar).
acceptanceCriteria: User holds mic button, says "make this paragraph shorter", releases — AI shows inline diff within 3 seconds
Speech recognition begins within 200ms of activation
Transcription shown in real-time as user speaks
Voice commands work on current selection (if selected) or current paragraph
All voice-initiated changes show inline diff preview — never modifies without confirmation
Works offline using on-device speech recognition (iOS 17+)
Cmd+Shift+J keyboard shortcut activates voice on iPad with external keyboard
VoiceOver users can distinguish between VoiceOver navigation and voice editing commands
Chained voice commands (speak, accept, speak again) work fluidly without re-activating

items.title: SPIKE: TextKit 2 Keystroke Latency Validation
items.intent: Validate that TextKit 2 can achieve <16ms keystroke-to-render latency before committing to it as the text engine
items.description: Build a minimal TextKit 2 prototype with NSTextLayoutManager and NSTextContentStorage. Benchmark keystroke-to-render latency with attributed string updates per paragraph on iPhone 15 and iPhone SE (3rd gen). Measure with os_signpost. If TextKit 2 fails the <16ms target, document findings and evaluate TextKit 1 fallback. Blocks FEAT-039 (Text Engine). Updates architecture decision [A-004].
acceptanceCriteria: Minimal TextKit 2 prototype runs on iPhone 15 and iPhone SE
Keystroke-to-render latency measured via os_signpost for 100+ consecutive keystrokes
Results documented: p50, p95, p99 latency values
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-001.md
Architecture decision [A-004] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: swift-markdown Round-Trip Fidelity
items.intent: Validate that swift-markdown can parse markdown, allow AST modifications, and re-emit markdown that preserves original formatting
items.description: Build a test harness that parses markdown via swift-markdown, modifies specific AST nodes (e.g. change heading level, update link URL, rewrite paragraph text), and re-emits markdown. Compare output to expected results. Test with 100+ CommonMark spec examples plus custom GFM cases (tables, task lists, strikethrough). Document any formatting loss. Blocks FEAT-038 (Parser). Updates architecture decision [A-003].
acceptanceCriteria: Test harness runs parse → modify → re-emit on 100+ spec examples
Formatting preservation rate documented (target: >95% of cases)
Known failure cases cataloged with workarounds
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-002.md
Architecture decision [A-003] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: swift-markdown Incremental Parsing
items.intent: Evaluate whether swift-markdown supports incremental parsing and design the debounce + local-update strategy for large documents
items.description: Evaluate swift-markdown for partial/incremental re-parse capability. Benchmark full re-parse time on a 10,000-line markdown document on iPhone 15. If incremental parse is not feasible, design and validate the debounce strategy: full re-parse on ~300ms editing pause on background thread, with lightweight per-paragraph local attribute updates between parses for <16ms keystroke response. Blocks FEAT-038 (Parser). Updates architecture decisions [A-003] and [A-017].
acceptanceCriteria: Full re-parse time for 10,000-line document measured on iPhone 15
Incremental parse feasibility documented (supported or not)
If not incremental: debounce + local-update strategy validated with prototype
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-003.md
Architecture decisions [A-003] and [A-017] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: The Render Animation Feasibility
items.intent: Validate the snapshot-based Core Animation approach for The Render signature transition at 120fps
items.description: Prototype the snapshot-based Core Animation approach for The Render (source-to-rich text transition). Capture layer snapshots, compute target positions, animate with CASpringAnimation (~400ms, damping 0.8, response 0.4). Measure frame rate on iPad Pro (ProMotion) and iPhone SE. Test with documents of varying sizes (10 lines, 100 lines, 1000 lines). Test Reduced Motion crossfade path. Blocks FEAT-014 (Signature Transition). Updates architecture decision [A-020].
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype demonstrates source-to-rich animation with at least heading and bold transitions
Frame rate measured on iPad Pro (target: 120fps) and iPhone SE (target: 60fps)
Reduced Motion crossfade (200ms) alternative tested
Performance with 1000-line document documented
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-004.md
Architecture decision [A-020] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: Local AI Inference Benchmarks and Device Capability Detection
items.intent: Benchmark MLX Swift vs Core ML for on-device inference, validate device capability detection, and select the local AI framework
items.description: Run inference benchmarks comparing MLX Swift and Core ML on A16 (iPhone 15) and M1 hardware. Test with 1-4GB quantized language models suitable for writing assistance. Measure: first token latency (target <500ms per D-PERF-4), tokens/sec throughput, peak memory usage, and memory-mapped model behavior. Additionally, validate the device capability detection approach (ProcessInfo hardware model → chip family mapping) to correctly identify A16+/M1+ devices. Verify App Store compliance for the model download and inference approach. Blocks FEAT-041 (AI Pipeline). Updates architecture decisions [A-008] and [A-033].
acceptanceCriteria: MLX Swift and Core ML benchmarked on A16 and M1 hardware
First token latency, tokens/sec, and memory measured for each
Recommended framework selected with rationale
App Store compliance for model download approach verified
Device capability detection (A16+/M1+ identification) validated on multiple device models
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-005.md
Architecture decisions [A-008] and [A-033] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: Mermaid WKWebView Memory Impact
items.intent: Validate the offscreen WKWebView approach for Mermaid rendering and measure memory impact with multiple diagrams
items.description: Prototype Mermaid diagram rendering using mermaid.js in an offscreen WKWebView. Render SVG output, convert to cached UIImage. Measure memory usage with 1, 5, and 10 diagrams in a single document. Test cache invalidation on content change. Test theme switching (light/dark). Evaluate WKWebView lifecycle (create/destroy vs reuse). Blocks FEAT-030 (Mermaid Rendering). Updates architecture decision [A-006].
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype renders at least 3 Mermaid diagram types (flowchart, sequence, ER)
Memory usage measured with 1, 5, and 10 diagrams
Cache strategy validated (content hash → image)
Theme switching (light/dark) tested
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-006.md
Architecture decision [A-006] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: tree-sitter Swift Integration
items.intent: Evaluate swift-tree-sitter package for syntax highlighting of fenced code blocks and measure binary size impact
items.description: Evaluate the swift-tree-sitter (or SwiftTreeSitter) package for integration into the EMEditor package. Build a prototype that highlights 3 languages (Swift, Python, JavaScript). Measure: binary size impact of bundling grammars, parse performance on a 500-line code block, and API ergonomics for incremental updates. Assess build system compatibility with SPM. Blocks FEAT-006 (Syntax Highlighting). Updates architecture decision [A-005].
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype highlights Swift, Python, and JavaScript code blocks
Binary size impact of 3 grammars measured (and extrapolated for 15+)
Parse performance on 500-line code block measured
SPM build compatibility verified
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-007.md
Architecture decision [A-005] updated based on findings

items.title: SPIKE: Apple Platform AI — WWDC 2026 Evaluation
items.intent: Evaluate Apple platform AI APIs after WWDC 2026 and prototype ApplePlatformAIProvider if APIs are available
items.description: Scoped to the WWDC 2026 evaluation window (June 2026). After WWDC, review all new Apple developer documentation for system-level AI APIs (on-device LLM, text generation, writing assistance APIs). If relevant APIs are announced: prototype the ApplePlatformAIProvider implementation against the AIProvider protocol, measure performance, and assess whether it replaces or supplements LocalModelProvider. If no relevant APIs: document findings and close the spike. Updates architecture decisions [A-007] and [A-029].
acceptanceCriteria: WWDC 2026 sessions and documentation reviewed for on-device AI APIs
Finding documented: APIs available (with prototype) or not available (with rationale)
If APIs available: prototype ApplePlatformAIProvider with performance measurements
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-008.md
Architecture decisions [A-007] and [A-029] updated based on findings
Spike closed with clear resolution (adopt, defer, or not applicable)

items.title: SPIKE: libgit2 Swift Bindings for iOS
items.intent: Evaluate libgit2 Swift bindings for git operations on iOS — clone, commit, push — to validate the GitHub storage approach
items.description: Evaluate SwiftGit2 or similar libgit2 Swift wrapper for iOS. Build a prototype that clones a GitHub repo, modifies a file, commits, and pushes. Measure: binary size impact, clone performance on Wi-Fi and cellular, commit+push latency, and SPM build compatibility. Verify App Store compliance (libgit2 is used by Working Copy and other shipped apps). Assess OAuth device flow for GitHub auth. Blocks FEAT-070, FEAT-071, FEAT-072 (GitHub Storage). Updates architecture decision [A-064].
acceptanceCriteria: Prototype clones a GitHub repo, modifies a file, commits, and pushes successfully
Binary size impact of libgit2 measured
Clone and push performance measured on Wi-Fi and cellular
SPM build compatibility verified
GitHub OAuth device flow prototyped and working
Written finding committed to docs/spikes/SPIKE-009.md
Architecture decision [A-064] updated based on findings

items.title: GitHub Storage — Clone and Open
items.intent: Users can open markdown files directly from their GitHub repos with the same ease as opening from iCloud or Dropbox
items.description: Phase 2. 'Open from GitHub' option on home screen alongside 'Open File'. User authenticates via GitHub OAuth device flow (one-time). Browses their repos, selects a file. EMGit clones the repo (or pulls if already cloned) to a local app container directory. File opens in the editor via EMFile — editing is identical to any local file. Cloned repos cached locally; repos not accessed in 30 days offered for cleanup in Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: User taps Open from GitHub, authenticates once, browses repos, and opens a .md file
File opens in the editor and is editable immediately — identical to opening from iCloud
Re-opening the same file uses the local clone without re-cloning
Clone progress shown for first-time repos
User without GitHub account sees no GitHub option (graceful absence)
Large repo (>100MB) shows progress and does not freeze the UI

items.title: GitHub Storage — Auto-Commit and Push
items.intent: Changes to files in GitHub repos are automatically committed and pushed so the user never has to think about git operations
items.description: Phase 2. When user closes a file or the app backgrounds: auto-save fires, then EMGit commits the change ('Update filename.md') and pushes to the remote. Pull-with-rebase before push to detect conflicts. If push fails (network, conflict): non-modal banner, changes remain committed locally and push on next opportunity. Subtle toolbar indicator shows when there are unpushed local commits. User can tap the indicator to push manually at any time without closing the file. Conflict on pull surfaces via the same external-change flow as file conflict detection (FEAT-045).
acceptanceCriteria: User edits a file from a GitHub repo, backgrounds the app — changes are committed and pushed automatically
Push failure shows non-modal banner — changes are safe in local commit
Unpushed changes indicator appears in toolbar when local commits exist
User can tap indicator to push manually before closing
Conflict during pull shows reload-or-keep prompt (same as FEAT-045)
Auto-commit message is sensible: 'Update filename.md'
Network offline: changes accumulate locally, push when back online

items.title: GitHub Storage — Auth and Repo Browser
items.intent: GitHub authentication must be seamless (one-time OAuth) and repo browsing must feel as natural as the system file picker
items.description: Phase 2. GitHub OAuth device flow: user enters a code at github.com/login/device — no web view required in the app. Token stored in Keychain (not UserDefaults). Token refresh handled automatically. Sign out available in Settings. Repo browser: list user's repos (personal + orgs), search/filter, show last updated date. Navigate into repo to browse .md files. Repo list cached for offline access to previously cloned repos.
acceptanceCriteria: OAuth device flow completes without a web view embedded in the app
Token persists in Keychain across app launches
Repo browser shows personal and org repos
User can search/filter repos by name
Sign out in Settings clears token and offers to delete local clones
Expired token triggers re-auth flow automatically

_dependsOn_docs: Items may include an optional 'dependsOn' array of item ID strings (e.g. "dependsOn": ["FEAT-001"]). Auto-pick skips items whose dependencies are not yet done.
